settings.title: Inside the Cold Room Before the Doors Open
settings.excerpt: Our florist Nadia conditions every stem by hand at dawn so nothing reaches you tired.
settings.body:
The flowers in our window look effortless, but getting them there starts in the cold room well before opening.

Nadia, who has arranged for us since the first month, conditions every delivery by hand. That means stripping lower leaves, cutting each stem, and letting them drink deep in cool water for hours.

Only then do they go out front. She says flowers are like people first thing in the morning. Give them water and a little quiet, and they come around.
